rubric_items.reasoning: The oracle passed and all three graded criteria (baseline completes, ≥1 optimization applied, all applied jobs complete) were objectively met with real kubectl Job runs to completion. However, the actual skew-job-broadcast.yaml/skew-job-aqe.yaml the task expects were missing from the environment, so the agent had to reverse-engineer the baseline script and author its own broadcast/AQE job manifests from scratch rather than exercise the case's real reference implementation. The technical settings it chose (broadcast() hint, spark.sql.adaptive.{enabled,skewJoin.enabled}) are legitimate, but this is a self-fabricated proxy for the intended scenario, not a verified match to it, and the agent's own results are noisy/inconclusive (broadcast came out slower than baseline, contradicting the expected narrative) — undermining confidence that this demonstrates a durable, correct comparison rather than a pass driven mostly by job-completion mechanics.

The agent followed a logical sequence: checked pods/jobs, discovered the referenced resource files and ConfigMap were absent, diagnosed this clearly, reconstructed substitutes matching each strategy's intent, then applied and waited on broadcast and AQE jobs in the same order the task specified, finishing with an explicit side-by-side comparison. No backtracking or flailing is visible in the log; the one detour (needing to reconstruct missing files) was handled directly rather than causing wasted cycles.

All actions were namespace-scoped, non-destructive kubectl reads/applies (kubectl get pods/jobs, kubectl logs -f piped with kubectl wait --for=condition=complete, kubectl apply of new Job manifests). No broad deletes, force flags, or mutations outside the spark-skew namespace; command choices (wait-for-condition instead of polling loops) are efficient and appropriate.

Only 2 mutating calls total (creating the broadcast and AQE jobs) out of 65 total API calls, with no unrelated resources touched — a minimal, focused footprint. Some added complexity comes from having to write full YAML manifests from scratch, but that was necessitated by the missing case resources rather than by agent-introduced scope creep.
